Title: xProfile — createOrganization Rejects XSS in Org Name
Preconditions: Runner-native (executes via scripts/graphql-runner.ts — no browser). USER credentials resolvable from .env. {{BACK_URL}}/graphql reachable. No organization named "AT&T Corp. #1" exists (cleanup in this case + prior runs handle prior state).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=valid_name] errors[] empty — OrganizationNameValidationPattern ^[\p{L}\p{M}\p{N}\s'\-\.&#/,()]+$ permits &, #, digits (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=valid_name] data.createOrganization.name = {{valid_org_name}}
[DATA label=valid_name] data.createOrganization.id is non-empty GUID
[ERRORS label=xss_name] errors[] non-empty — input validation rejects <, > characters (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=xss_name] data.createOrganization is null (no org created with script tags in name)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] GET /api/members/{{VALID_ORG_ID}} returns 200 (valid org persisted)
[ROUNDTRIP label=valid_name] re-query organization(id: {{VALID_ORG_ID}}) returns matching name
Failure_Signals: Schema validator rejects mutation pre-send; 5xx from /graphql; errors[] empty on XSS payload (validation regression); data non-null on xss_name (script-tag name accepted)

Title: xProfile — updateContact Rejects HTML and Oversized Phone Values
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated; their contact record exists with non-empty firstName/lastName (runner fetches both before issuing mutations so InputUpdateContactType required fields are preserved).
Assertions: [ERRORS label=html_phone] errors[] non-empty — EnableNoHtmlTagsValidation rejects tag (BL-GQL-001) [DATA label=html_phone] data.updateContact is null [ERRORS label=oversized_phone] errors[] non-empty — MaxLength(64) on phones rejects 65-char input (BL-GQL-001) [DATA label=oversized_phone] data.updateContact is null [ERRORS label=valid_phone] errors[] empty — valid E.164-adjacent phone accepted [DATA label=valid_phone] data.updateContact.id is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=valid_phone] runner-manual: re-query contact(id: {{CONTACT_ID}}) after updates confirms phones contains {{valid_phone}}
Failure_Signals: Schema validator rejects mutation pre-send; 5xx from /graphql; errors[] empty on HTML or oversized payloads (validation regression); data non-null on html_phone or oversized_phone
Cleanup: none — valid_phone mutation restores the contact to a known-good phone value
References: VCST-4691 PR #130; VCST-4803 PR #132 (MaxLength 64 + NoHtmlTags on phones)

Title: xProfile — updatePersonalData Validates Name Fields
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Captures original firstName/lastName at setup, exercises XSS rejection + Unicode acceptance, then restores original values in inline cleanup — no state pollution on the shared ORG_USER account.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=setup_original] errors[] empty
[DATA label=setup_original] data.me.contact.firstName is non-null
[DATA label=setup_original] data.me.contact.lastName is non-null
[ERRORS label=xss_name] errors[] non-empty — NameValidationPattern rejects <script> in firstName (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=xss_name] data.updatePersonalData is null
[ERRORS label=valid_unicode] errors[] empty — Unicode names (accents, apostrophes, hyphens) accepted
[DATA label=valid_unicode] data.updatePersonalData.succeeded = true
[ERRORS label=cleanup_restore] errors[] empty
[DATA label=cleanup_restore] data.updatePersonalData.succeeded = true
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=valid_unicode] runner-manual: re-query me { contact { firstName lastName } } confirms Unicode names persisted before cleanup_restore
Failure_Signals: Schema validator rejects mutation pre-send; 5xx from /graphql; errors[] empty on XSS payload (validation regression); valid_unicode.succeeded = false (false positive on valid Unicode input); cleanup_restore fails (account drift — original values stuck as François/O'Brien-Müller)

Title: xProfile — requestRegistration Duplicate Email Rejected
Preconditions: Runner-native. Anonymous requestRegistration with a known-existing email (ORG_USER_EMAIL from .env). Expects result.succeeded=false with DuplicateEmail/DuplicateUserName errors.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=dup_register] errors[] empty — server returns structured validation via result.errors, not top-level errors
[DATA label=dup_register] data.requestRegistration.result.succeeded = false
[DATA label=dup_register] data.requestRegistration.contact is null
[DATA label=dup_register] data.requestRegistration.account is null

Failure_Signals: result.succeeded=true on duplicate (P0 data corruption); HTTP 500; stack trace in errors[]; account created

Title: xProfile — me + UserType Schema Coverage
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Runner's schema validator rejects the query pre-send if any requested field is renamed/removed — this test is a schema-drift canary for UserType. NOTE: ContactType traversal (addresses / organizations / securityAccounts connections) needs Connection-wrapper handling (items { ... }) and is tracked as a separate migration (GQL-046b follow-up).
Steps:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP schema_coverage]
  query {
    me {
      id
      userName
      email
      emailConfirmed
      photoUrl
      phoneNumber
      phoneNumberConfirmed
      twoFactorEnabled
      isAdministrator
      passwordExpired
      memberId
      permissions
    }
  }
[GQL-EXEC schema_coverage]
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=schema_coverage] errors[] empty — every UserType field resolves against the live schema (BL-GQL-002)
[DATA label=schema_coverage] data.me.id is non-null
[DATA label=schema_coverage] data.me.userName is non-null
[DATA label=schema_coverage] data.me.memberId is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=schema_coverage] runner-manual: re-execute; response shape stable across calls
Failure_Signals: Runner exits with DV-009 (field renamed/removed) at schema-validate stage; errors[] non-empty on any sub-selection
Cleanup: none — pure read, no state change

Title: xProfile — updateMemberAddresses Duplicate-Insert Semantics
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures the user's existing address list, then calls updateMemberAddresses twice with the same test address to verify REPLACE semantics (second call is an identity no-op). Inline cleanup_restore reverts the address list to its pre-test state — no account pollution.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=insert_first] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=insert_again] errors[] empty
[DATA label=insert_again] data.updateMemberAddresses.id is non-null
[ERRORS label=cleanup_restore] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Post-both-inserts: currentCustomerAddresses should contain the test address once, not twice. updateMemberAddresses uses REPLACE — second call is identity. NOTE: cleanup_restore wipes the user's address list to empty []; capture_original is captured for evidence/debugging but cannot be replayed in a single REPLACE call due to runner-MVP lacking object-array round-trip from capture. Operator should manually re-seed addresses for the test user if non-empty original is required for downstream tests.
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; duplicate address persisted (2 records with same line1+city+postalCode); cleanup_restore fails (would leave test address on account)
Cleanup: none — inline cleanup_restore empties address list

Title: [VCST-5073] currentCustomerAddresses — ids Filter Returns Single Matching Address
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Queries currentCustomerAddresses without filter first to capture an existing address id, then re-queries with that id via the ids arg (comma-separated String). Schema: currentCustomerAddresses(ids: String) — arg is ids (plural), type String. SUITE-ORDERING: ORG_USER must have at least one address on file. This case MUST run BEFORE PRF-GQL-056 (whose cleanup_restore wipes the address list to [] — runner-MVP limitation pending object-array restore). If running standalone or after PRF-GQL-056, manually re-seed an address via Admin SPA → Contacts before invoking, or expect the [GQL-CAPTURE list_all.data.me.addresses.0.id] to fail.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=list_all] errors[] empty
[DATA label=list_all] data.currentCustomerAddresses.items is non-null
[ERRORS label=filter_by_id] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=filter_by_id] data.currentCustomerAddresses.totalCount = 1
[DATA label=filter_by_id] data.currentCustomerAddresses.items.0.id = {{ADDR_ID}}
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] captured ADDR_ID from list_all; filter_by_id returns exactly that record
Failure_Signals: list_all errors[] non-empty; ADDR_ID not captured (user has no addresses); filter_by_id returns 0 or >1 items; id mismatch
References: VCST-5073; xAPI; Query.currentCustomerAddresses; Synced VCST-5073 fix: arg name id→ids (TLC-2026-05-15-2148, 2026-05-15)
